TestCase.Name: Production build and lint checks
TestCase.Evidence: npm run lint passed; npm run build passed.

TestCase.Name: Signup success
TestCase.Evidence: POST /api/auth/signup returned 201 with {"success":true,"message":"Account created successfully."} in dev mode.

TestCase.Name: Duplicate signup
TestCase.Evidence: Repeated signup returned 409 with {"success":false,"message":"Account already exists."}.

TestCase.Name: Invalid login credentials
TestCase.Evidence: POST /api/auth/login with wrong password returned 401 with {"success":false,"message":"Invalid email or password."}.

TestCase.Name: Login success and session persistence
TestCase.Evidence: Login returned 200 and set auth_token cookie; subsequent GET /api/auth/me returned the authenticated user payload.

TestCase.Name: Logout and session invalidation
TestCase.Evidence: POST /api/auth/logout returned 200, cleared auth_token, and subsequent GET /api/auth/me returned 401 when the cookie jar was updated like a browser.

TestCase.Name: Unauthorized API access
TestCase.Evidence: GET /api/auth/me and GET /api/notes without a session returned 401 Unauthorized.

TestCase.Name: Signup validation
TestCase.Evidence: POST /api/auth/signup with short name, bad email, and short password returned 400 with field-level validation errors.

TestCase.Name: Login validation
TestCase.Evidence: POST /api/auth/login missing password returned 400 with a password validation error.

TestCase.Name: Create note
TestCase.Evidence: POST /api/notes returned 201 and created note records with the expected note payload.

TestCase.Name: Edit note
TestCase.Evidence: PUT /api/notes/{id} returned 200 and updated the note title, description, priority, and updatedAt timestamp.

TestCase.Name: Delete note
TestCase.Evidence: DELETE /api/notes/{id} returned 200 and removed the note from subsequent list/dashboard checks.

TestCase.Name: Search and priority filter
TestCase.Evidence: GET /api/notes?search=Alpha returned the matching note; GET /api/notes?priority=LOW returned an empty set after the remaining note was MEDIUM.

TestCase.Name: Dashboard stats synchronization
TestCase.Evidence: GET /api/dashboard reflected note mutations immediately with totalNotes/highPriority/mediumPriority/lowPriority counts matching the database state.

TestCase.Name: Ownership enforcement
TestCase.Evidence: A second user attempting PUT /api/notes/{id} on another user's note received 403 Forbidden.

TestCase.Name: Not-found handling
TestCase.Evidence: Authenticated DELETE /api/notes/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 returned 404 with {"success":false,"message":"Note not found."}.

TestCase.Name: Production server auth flow

StepsToReproduce.Step: Run npm run start -- --hostname 127.0.0.1 --port 3000 with the provided local .env.

TestCase.ExpectedResult: Signup should create the account and return 201.
TestCase.ActualResult: The route returned 500 Internal Server Error with {"success":false,"message":"An unexpected error occurred."} and the server logged that rate limiting is not configured because UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN are missing in production.
TestCase.SuggestedFix: Make production startup resilient when Upstash rate limiting is unavailable, or require/document the env vars before starting production. A local/prod-safe fallback should be selected without crashing auth routes.

TestCase.Name: Server-side protection for dashboard route

StepsToReproduce.Step: Request GET /dashboard without a session cookie.

TestCase.ExpectedResult: Unauthenticated users should be redirected to /login or receive an unauthorized response before any protected shell is rendered.
TestCase.ActualResult: The route returned 200 HTML containing the dashboard shell, sidebar, header, and loading skeleton, which means protection is happening only after client hydration.
TestCase.SuggestedFix: Move the auth gate to the server layer or middleware so unauthenticated requests are redirected before the protected UI shell is rendered.

Bug.Title: Auth routes crash in production because local Upstash config is missing

StepsToReproduce.Step: Start the app with npm run start.
Submit valid signup or login data.

Bug.ExpectedResult: Auth requests should succeed in the local production server or fail with a clear configuration message before runtime.
Bug.ActualResult: Signup returned 500 and the server logged a rate-limiting configuration error.
Bug.SuggestedFix: Provide a production-safe fallback when Upstash env vars are absent, or enforce and document the required production configuration separately from local QA.

Bug.Title: Protected routes are not blocked server-side
Bug.ExpectedResult: Unauthenticated users should not receive the protected dashboard shell and should be redirected or denied immediately.
Bug.ActualResult: The route served a 200 response with the full app shell and loading skeleton.
Bug.SuggestedFix: Use server-side session verification and redirect unauthenticated traffic before rendering protected layouts.

Recommendations.Recommendation: Separate local QA configuration from production startup requirements so auth can be verified without hidden external services.
Add server-side route guarding for dashboard, notes, and settings to avoid protected-shell flashes and tighten access control.
Keep the current API contract tests in a lightweight automated suite because the runtime behavior is sensitive to environment settings.

ProductionReadiness.Reason: Core auth works in development, but the production server path fails without Upstash rate limiting config and protected routes are only client-gated.

Blockers.Blocker: Auth endpoints must not 500 on a standard local production startup.
Protected routes should be enforced before the protected shell is rendered.
